schema.colMultiplier: Multiplier applied to user's USD baseline spending. 1.00 = US baseline.
schema.annualHealthcareUSD: Additive baseline annual healthcare cost in USD for a retired adult / small family. Conservative midpoint.
schema.withdrawalTaxRate: Approximate effective tax rate on retirement withdrawals for a moderate-income retiree. Heuristic — not a substitute for individualized tax modeling.
schema.swr: Suggested safe withdrawal rate baseline for this country.
schema.confidence: high | medium | low — reflects how well-supported the parameter set is by public data.

countries.name: United States
countries.residencyNote: Default base case. Healthcare estimate assumes pre-Medicare ACA marketplace coverage for a retired couple.
caveats: Withdrawal tax rate assumes mixed taxable/IRA/Roth portfolio at a moderate income level. Heavy pre-tax skews higher; heavy Roth skews lower.

countries.name: South Korea
countries.residencyNote: F-2 long-term residency or F-5 permanent residency pathways exist. No dedicated retirement visa; pathway typically requires prior residency, marriage, or qualifying income.

sources.name: Numbeo cost-of-living index (Seoul vs US average)
Korea National Health Insurance Service rate schedule

caveats: COL varies substantially Seoul vs regional cities (±25%).
Tax treatment of foreign retirement accounts and Roth distributions is jurisdiction-specific; consult a cross-border tax specialist.
Visa path is the practical constraint for most US citizens, not the math.

countries.name: Japan
countries.residencyNote: No dedicated retirement visa. Long-term resident or spouse-of-Japanese visa are common pathways. Japan Highly Skilled Professional visa accelerates permanent residency for qualifying workers.

sources.name: Numbeo cost-of-living index (Tokyo vs US average)
Japan National Health Insurance premium schedule

caveats: Japan taxes worldwide income for residents after 5 of last 10 years in country. Pre-residency window has lighter treatment.
Tokyo COL is meaningfully higher than regional Japan.
Inheritance tax exposure for long-term residents is significant — not modeled here.

countries.name: Taiwan
countries.residencyNote: Taiwan Gold Card available for qualifying professionals; APRC (permanent residency) achievable after sustained residency. Foreign-sourced income receives favorable treatment for new residents.

sources.name: Numbeo cost-of-living index (Taipei vs US average)
Taiwan National Health Insurance premium schedule
Taiwan Ministry of Finance tax guidance for foreign residents

caveats: Foreign-source income (including US retirement accounts) generally enjoys favorable treatment — modeled here as low effective rate, but jurisdiction-specific.
NHI is one of the cheapest comprehensive systems in the developed world.
Cross-strait political risk is not modeled — outside the scope of a FIRE calculator.
